name: Mixed-Method Market Research Framework
purpose: Combine public context with founder-led research and experiments so market claims remain traceable and useful.
questionsToAsk: Which evidence is local enough to guide this decision?
What behavior has been observed instead of assumed?
Which independent source could challenge the current conclusion?
scoringModel: Score source quality, recency, geographic specificity, industry specificity, sample size, primary-research availability, secondary-data availability, independent-source count, and consistency.
Reduce confidence when a result depends on one source.
Keep mock values outside confidence calculations.
Label derived estimates and record their assumptions.
qualityChecks: Store source URLs and retrieval dates.
Do not present sample data as official.
warnings: Public statistics add context but do not prove customer demand.
Internet research should respect source terms and favor official APIs.
